steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states “evaluations varied widely depending on which occupation was being considered.” The example must compare different occupations to demonstrate this variation.

steps.title: Match the contrast across occupations
steps.content:
82% of participants believe that it is somewhat or very likely that a robot could work effectively as a tour guide, but only 16% believe that it is somewhat or very likely that a robot could work as a surgeon. presents tour guide at 82% likely versus surgeon at only 16% likely—a dramatic difference between occupations that directly illustrates the variation.

Two other options provide single-occupation data without comparison, while B highlights matching percentages rather than variation.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
